document.title: Usability Validation Plan for B1417MCW / B1717MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: EN 60601-1-6:2010 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Usability
EN 62366-1:2015 Medical devices – Application of usability engineering to medical Devices
IEC TR 62366-2:2016 Medical devices — Part 2: Guidance on the application of usability engineering to medical devices
EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical Devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation test plan for B1417MCW/B1717MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during development, including participants, procedures, metrics, tasks, acceptance criteria, goals, reporting, and references.
keyPoints: The document states the B1417MCW and B1717MCW accessories have the same performance and usage except for size, with no difference in usability.
A total of 7 participants should match the stated user profile for education, legal qualification, English understanding, and experience.
Usability metrics include scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
Hazard-related use scenarios cover user manual review, product preparation, product use, X-ray image implementation, maintenance, cleaning, and battery charging/change.
The goal for each usability task is a total completion rate of 100% and total errors below 4.
A usability verification and evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.

concepts.name: Usability validation plan
concepts.evidence: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of the B1417MCW/B1717MCW.

concepts.name: Usability metrics
concepts.evidence: Usability metrics refers to user performance measured against specific performance goals necessary to satisfy usability requirements.

concepts.name: Scenario completion
concepts.evidence: Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, and subjective evaluations will be used.

concepts.name: Critical errors
concepts.evidence: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

concepts.name: Non-critical errors
concepts.evidence: Non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

concepts.name: Subjective evaluations
concepts.evidence: Subjective opinions about specific tasks, time to perform each task, features, and functionality will be surveyed and collected via questionnaires.

concepts.name: Hazard-related use scenarios
concepts.evidence: hazard-related use scenarios for summative evaluation

concepts.name: Completion rate goal
concepts.evidence: Usability Goal: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal for each usability task.

concepts.name: Usability engineering
concepts.evidence: EN 62366-1:2015 Medical devices – Application of usability engineering to medical Devices

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical Devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The document is a usability validation plan for B1417MCW/B1717MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Plan for B1417MCW / B1717MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-10-106 and Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-10-106 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The document date is Oct 01, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Park, Jaehong Oct 01, 2024; Review R&D Manager Lee, Dongho Oct 01, 2024; Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Oct 01, 2024

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was established on Oct 01, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Rev. date Oct 01, 2024 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The document describes a usability test plan during development of B1417MCW/B1717MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of the B1417MCW/B1717MCW.

evidence.claim: The usability test objectives include benchmarking user attributes, identifying design problems, improving design and production efficiency, and maximizing end-user satisfaction.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability test objectives are: To construct the benchmarks of user attributes; To identify potential design problems to be solve; To improve product design and production efficiency; To complete end-user satisfaction maximization

evidence.claim: B1417MCW and B1717MCW have the same performance and usage except for size, with no usability difference stated.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same except for size, so there is no difference in 'Usability'

evidence.claim: The usability test uses a total of 7 participants matching the stated user profile.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants should be at least equal to the following User Profile used in the Usability test.

evidence.claim: Participants should have at least two years of university education, meet legal qualifications for each country, understand English, and require no special experience.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: At least two years of university, equal or more; Knowledge: User who meet the legal qualification for each country; Language understanding: User who are understand the English; Experience: Not require any special experience

evidence.claim: Testing is conducted in a testing lab in a typical operating environment described in the usability engineering report.
evidence.evidenceText: Participants will take part in the usability test at the testing lab. The B1417MCW/B1717MCW will be used in a typical operating environment described in usability engineering report.

evidence.claim: Participants sign informed consent acknowledging voluntary participation and ability to cease participation at any time.
evidence.evidenceText: Participants will sign an informed consent that acknowledges: the participation is voluntary, that participation can cease at any time.

evidence.claim: Usability metrics include scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
evidence.evidenceText: Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, and subjective evaluations will be used. Time-to-completion of scenarios will also be collected.

evidence.claim: Critical errors include unresolved errors during task completion or errors that produce an incorrect safety-related outcome.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

evidence.claim: Non-critical errors do not affect final task output but reduce task efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

evidence.claim: Post-test satisfaction uses a 5-point rating scale from strongly disagree to strongly agree.
evidence.evidenceText: Rating: The 5-point rating scale ranged from 1 (Strongly disagree) to 5 (Strongly agree)

evidence.claim: Primary operating function scenarios include manual familiarization, power and cable connection, detector power-on, patient arrangement, and detector calibration by PC software.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the primary operating functions. Familiarize yourself with the user manual... Connecting the power cord... LAN Cable and PC main body connection... Power on the detector... Arrange the patient... Calibration of detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: Hazard-related use scenarios include safety information comprehension, connection errors, detector power-on, calibration, indicators, X-ray image implementation, cleaning, and battery charging/change.
evidence.evidenceText: hazard-related use scenarios for summative evaluation... Check the User manual... Preparation for using the product... Product use... Implementation of X-ray image... Maintenance... Cleaning... Battery charging and change

evidence.claim: The usability goal for each task is a total completion rate of 100%.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: The usability goal for errors is total errors below 4 for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: Total errors of below 4 is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: A usability verification and evaluation report will be provided after the usability test.
evidence.evidenceText: The Usability verification & evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.

evidence.claim: The document references EN 60601-1-6:2010, EN 62366-1:2015, IEC TR 62366-2:2016, and EN ISO 14971:2019.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly stated in the source text; authoritySignal is only 'general'.
Source metadata productSignal is 'Unclassified', although the source text identifies B1417MCW and B1717MCW product models.
Several acceptance criteria state 'over 100% of operators', which is mathematically ambiguous and may require source document review.
